drugs.notes: Cycle 1 only: 90mg once daily days 1-7, then escalate to 180mg once daily days 8-28. From cycle 2 onwards: 180mg once daily days 1-28. Available as 30mg, 90mg and 180mg tablets. Swallow whole with water, with or without food. Avoid grapefruit/grapefruit juice. If interrupted 14+ days (non-toxicity reason), resume at 90mg once daily for 7 days before increasing to previously tolerated dose. If strong CYP3A inhibitors unavoidable, reduce from 180mg to 90mg or 90mg to 60mg.
Cycle 1 only: dose escalation from 90mg (days 1-7) to 180mg (days 8-28). From cycle 2 onwards: 180mg once daily days 1-28.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 60mg once daily for days 1-7
Reduce to 60mg once daily. Permanently discontinue if unable to tolerate 60mg once daily.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 haematological toxicity (neutrophils <1x10^9/L, lymphocytes <0.5x10^9/L, or platelets <50x10^9/L) - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to baseline, then resume at same dose
Withhold until recovery to baseline then resume at next lower dose level or permanently discontinue
Withhold until recovery to baseline, then resume at next lower dose level
Reduce starting dose to 60mg once daily for first 7 days, then 120mg once daily

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 AST/ALT with bilirubin 2xULN or less
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to baseline or ≤3xULN AST/ALT then resume at next lower dose

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2+ AST/ALT with bilirubin >2xULN (no cholestasis or haemolysis)

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce starting dose to 60mg once daily for first 7 days then increase to 90mg once daily. Monitor closely for ILD/pneumonitis, especially in first week.
Withhold until recovery to baseline, then resume at same dose level. Do NOT escalate to 180mg.
Withhold until recovery to baseline, then resume at next lower dose level. Do NOT escalate to 180mg.
Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or below (SBP <140mmHg, DBP <90mmHg), then resume at same dose. If recurs, resume at next lower dose or permanently discontinue.
Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or below then resume at next lower dose or permanently discontinue. If recurs, permanently discontinue.

Withhold until asymptomatic or heart rate ≥60bpm. If causative concomitant medication identified and stopped, resume at same dose. If no causative concomitant medication or not discontinued, resume at next lower dose.
Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or baseline, then resume at same dose

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 CPK elevation - recurrence or Grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or baseline, then resume at next lower dose level
Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or baseline, then resume at same dose

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 lipase/amylase elevation - recurrence or Grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or baseline, then resume at next lower dose level

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3+ hyperglycaemia not controlled with optimal medical management
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until adequate hyperglycaemic control achieved, then resume at next lower dose or permanently discontinue
Withhold until recovery to grade 1 or baseline, then resume at next lower dose level

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral up to three times a day when required (cycle 1 take-home only)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2mg after each subsequent loose stool, maximum 16mg in 24 hours (cycle 1 take-home only)
special_warnings: ALK-positive NSCLC previously treated with crizotinib only; ECOG PS 0-2
ILD/pneumonitis risk: monitor closely for new or worsening respiratory symptoms (dyspnoea, cough) especially in the first 7 days - do NOT escalate to 180mg if Grade 1-2 ILD occurs in first 7 days
Dose escalation scheme: 90mg once daily days 1-7 of cycle 1, then 180mg once daily from day 8 onwards
Permanently discontinue if patient unable to tolerate 60mg once daily
Grapefruit/grapefruit juice must be avoided (increases plasma concentrations)
Avoid concomitant strong and moderate CYP3A inducers; reduce dose with strong CYP3A inhibitors
If dose missed or vomiting occurs, do not take additional dose - take next dose at scheduled time
Allow at least 7-day interval between discontinuation of crizotinib and start of brigatinib
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed
Must only be prescribed under supervision of a consultant oncologist
Bradycardia risk: monitor heart rate; increase monitoring frequency if concomitant bradycardia-causing drugs cannot be avoided
Treatment breaks of up to 6 weeks allowed to allow toxicities to settle

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose set at 890mg in ARIA (internationally recommended max 900mg at CrCl 125ml/min). AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance - seek consultant advice. Dose may need adjustment if GFR changes >10% from previous cycle.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts remain low, use 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs despite reduction, stop treatment.
Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If still low, use 20% dose reduction. If platelets fall below 50x10^9/L, reduce dose by 50%.
Do not administer if CrCl is less than 20ml/min

dose_modifications.condition: Significant GFR change >10% from previous cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Dose adjustment may be required; recalculate AUC-based dose

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or intravenous (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take-home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take-home)
special_warnings: Second line therapy of stage III or IV NSCLC; WHO PS 0-1; palliative intent
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before first cycle
AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance instead of calculated GFR - seek consultant advice
Carboplatin is an irritant - extravasation precautions required
Dose of carboplatin may need alteration if renal function changes >10% from previous cycle
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to each cycle; chest x-ray before each cycle

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 890mg in ARIA (internationally recommended max 900mg at CrCl 125ml/min). AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Dose may need adjustment if GFR changes >10%.

Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands. If bilirubin >27micromol/L, initiate at 800mg/m2. Consider dose adjustment if CrCl <30ml/min. Gemcitabine is neutral (extravasation).

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs, stop treatment.
Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs, stop treatment. Day 8 dose adjustments per local practice.
Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction. If platelets <50x10^9/L, reduce by 50%.
Initiate treatment with dose of 800mg/m2. AST elevations do not appear to cause dose-limiting toxicities.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or intravenous (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take-home, starting day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take-home)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or intravenous (day 8, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy only)
special_warnings: First line therapy of stage III or IV NSCLC; WHO PS 0-2; palliative intent
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before first cycle; FBC day 8 required
Carboplatin is an irritant; Gemcitabine is neutral - extravasation precautions required
Gemcitabine has radiosensitising properties
Dose of carboplatin may need alteration if renal function changes >10% from previous cycle
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required
Day 8 dose adjustments for gemcitabine should be made according to local practice guidelines

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 890mg in ARIA (internationally recommended max 900mg at CrCl 125ml/min). AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Consider dose reduction in poor performance patients. Carboplatin is an irritant.
Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (20mg/ml). Hypotension risk on rapid infusion. Etoposide is an irritant.

Oral etoposide taken once daily on days 2 and 3. Dose rounded to nearest 50mg (up if halfway). Take an hour before food or on an empty stomach. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, use 80% of last dose. If myelosuppression recurs, stop treatment.
Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, use 80% of last dose. If myelosuppression recurs, stop treatment.
Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still in range, continue using same approach.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin 26-51 micromol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units
Hepatic impairment: bilirubin >51 micromol/L or AST/ALT >180 units

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous bolus (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or intravenous bolus (day 1, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take-home, starting day 2 morning and lunch)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take-home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take-home, starting evening of day 1)
special_warnings: First line treatment of SCLC; WHO PS 0-3; palliative intent
SCLC can be very sensitive to chemotherapy - risk of tumour lysis syndrome. Consider allopurinol for at-risk patients starting the day before chemotherapy.
Etoposide IV on day 1; oral etoposide on days 2-3 - NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert MUST be adhered to for oral etoposide
Etoposide oral must be taken an hour before food or on an empty stomach
Hypotension risk with rapid IV etoposide infusion - administer over 60 minutes
Carboplatin and etoposide are both irritants - extravasation precautions required
AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance - seek consultant advice
Consider dose reduction in poor performance status patients
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes using a 0.2 micron low protein binding filter. Administer BEFORE carboplatin and pemetrexed. Given for cycles 1-35 (up to 2 years total treatment). Dose reductions NOT permitted - treatment may be interrupted or discontinued only. Neutral extravasation risk.

Intravenous infusion in 100ml glucose 5% or sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Given for all 35 cycles. Carboplatin should be administered 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion. Pemetrexed is an inflammitant. Dose banded per national dose bands (25NS). If CrCl <45ml/min consider dose reduction. Drain pleural/peritoneal effusions prior to administration.

Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Given for cycles 1-4 only. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 790mg in ARIA (internationally recommended max 750mg at CrCl 125ml/min). AUC4 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Start 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion. Carboplatin is an irritant.

dose_modifications.notes: Pembrolizumab dose reductions are NOT permitted. Treatment may only be interrupted or discontinued. Funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: platelets <49x10^9/L with NCI-CTC grade 2 bleeding
Haematological: platelets <49x10^9/L with NCI-CTC grade 2 bleeding

dose_modifications.notes: No dose reduction in mild hepatic impairment
Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. May restart when liver function at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab hepatitis: AST/ALT ≥5xULN or bilirubin >3xULN or grade 3+

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. May restart when reaction at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis or any grade 4 adverse reaction

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. May restart when colitis at grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

pre_medications: Folic acid 5mg once daily oral starting 1-2 weeks prior to pemetrexed and continuing for 3 weeks after last pemetrexed dose (MANDATORY)
Hydroxocobalamin (Vitamin B12) 1mg intramuscular injection every 3 months starting 1-2 weeks prior to pemetrexed (MANDATORY)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral starting day before pemetrexed for 3 days (skin rash prophylaxis - MANDATORY for pemetrexed)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous (cycles 1-4, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 20mg intravenous if patient forgot to take oral dexamethasone pre-medication
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or intravenous (cycles 5-35, 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take-home)
Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (as required for infusion-related reactions)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous (as required for infusion-related reactions)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (as required for infusion-related reactions)
special_warnings: Untreated PD-L1-positive or negative locally advanced or metastatic non-squamous NSCLC; EGFR and ALK mutation negative; PS 0-1
Pembrolizumab dose reductions are NOT permitted under any circumstances
Funding approval precludes further pembrolizumab after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer
Total treatment duration: 4 cycles carboplatin+pembrolizumab+pemetrexed, then up to 31 further cycles of pembrolizumab+pemetrexed maintenance (maximum 35 cycles / 2 years total)
Folic acid and B12 supplementation MANDATORY before pemetrexed - must start 1-2 weeks prior
Dexamethasone skin rash prophylaxis MANDATORY for pemetrexed (4mg twice daily for 3 days starting day before)
Pembrolizumab associated with immune-related adverse reactions: pneumonitis, nephritis, colitis, hepatitis, thyroid disorders, hypophysitis, endocrinopathy
Consider draining pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to pemetrexed administration
Pembrolizumab administered FIRST (before carboplatin and pemetrexed); carboplatin starts 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed
Pembrolizumab must be administered using a low protein binding (0.2 micron) filter
Carboplatin max dose 790mg (set at 790mg in ARIA); AUC4 may be considered with EDTA clearance
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration
Thyroid function tests required before each cycle

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 100ml glucose 5% or sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Administered BEFORE carboplatin. Dose banded per national dose bands. If CrCl <45ml/min consider dose reduction. Drain pleural/peritoneal effusions prior to administration. Pemetrexed is an inflammitant.

Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Start 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 890mg in ARIA (internationally recommended max 900mg at CrCl 125ml/min). AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Carboplatin is an irritant.

Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs, stop treatment.
Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction.

Pemetrexed administered BEFORE carboplatin; carboplatin starts 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion

Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 60mg. During concurrent radiotherapy use 15mg/m2; increase to 30mg/m2 when radiotherapy is complete. Vinorelbine is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required. For severe hepatic impairment consider reduction to 10mg/m2.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs, stop treatment.

Delay 7 days. If recovery, continue at initial dose. If still low, 20% dose reduction.
For intravenous preparation consider reduction to 10mg/m2 in severe liver impairment

Oral capsules (available as 20mg and 30mg). Maximum dose 120mg for 60mg/m2 dose. Dose rounded to nearest 20mg (up if halfway). Capsules must be swallowed whole with food, not chewed, sucked or dissolved. From cycle 2 onwards consider increasing to 80mg/m2 (maximum dose 160mg). Must NOT be supplied from primary care. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed. In moderate hepatic impairment consider 50mg/m2/week. For severe hepatic impairment (IV preparation): consider reduction to 20mg/m2.
